guest: Andrew Wilkinson, founder of Tiny Acquisitions, investor in niche software companies

business_ideas.idea: Shopify-adjacent SaaS software company for e-commerce, bought back and taken public
Carte: cap table management platform turned secondary stock marketplace for private companies
Pipe: upfront financing for SaaS contracts, turning contracts into liquid asset class
Rogue Fitness: bootstrapped weightlifting equipment, hiring 2000 during COVID
Callendly: scheduling tool at $70M ARR, bootstrapped by Nigerian immigrant
Moderna: mRNA vaccine development, 10x return this year
Zoom: held world together during pandemic, saved schools and families
Cryptocurrency as network effect investment, analyzing adoption metrics
Supercast: paid subscription podcast platform to monetize creator audiences

predictions.text: Bitcoin going from $400 to multiples higher based on network effect analysis and institutional adoption signals
Nobody will give a shit about COVID in a year, life will bounce back just like after 9-11
San Francisco will remain center of tech world despite government problems and will be rebuilt
Porn subscription model becomes mainstream, reversing free-to-paid trend of Pornhub era
Mark Leonard of Constellation Software worth $2 billion will remain anonymous and private long-term

disagreements: Sean and Shaan clashed on Bitcoin: Shaan bullish on network effects and institutional adoption signals; Sean skeptical of his own analysis skills vs traditional asset valuation, concerned retail investors copying him. Shaan defended Bitcoin as similar to early Facebook but for money. Fundamental disagreement on whether macro trades are legitimate vs real business analysis.
dynamics: This is an awards show format where each host nominates winners. Andrew drives deeper business analysis with specificity; Sam provides contrarian picks; Shaan defends Bitcoin conviction passionately. Dynamic is collaborative but Shaan clearly most engaged on crypto thesis. Andrew shares business strategies from his portfolio work.
frameworks: Billy of the Year (net worth milestone + gave fortune away)
Breakout company (new category, high growth, escaped velocity)
Under the radar company (benefiting but not yet visible)
Input vs output metrics (tracking workouts and inputs vs results)
The 1000-day rule of compounding (Mark Leonard example)
Second-order consequences (which businesses benefit indirectly from trend)
Network effect as investment thesis (Bitcoin adoption metrics)
learnings: Boring, unsexy business verticals with high switching costs compound value over decades better than trendy spaces
Bitcoin's strength lies in network adoption metrics and institutional capital flow, not price speculation alone
Most successful founders reinvest gains into lifestyle changes that enable compounding, not status consumption
Supply-side business models (Pipe financing, secondary markets) can unlock value faster than demand-side
Fitness is fundamentally a diet problem, not a workout problem; small dial adjustments beat aggressive yo-yo cycles
Amazon popularized measuring inputs over outputs; this framework applies beyond operations to personal progress

banger_quotes.quote: Money is one of the largest network effect projects that there is

If you understand businesses really well, Bitcoin is harder to analyze than GoPro vs Apple

notable: This is the 2020 Millies Awards show, the first formal awards ceremony. Andrew Wilkinson appears as guest and reveals strategy buying back sold company and taking public for $220M on $10M invested.
